title: Linear Algebra for CFD
subtitle: Sparse Matrix Systems for R410A Two-Phase Flow

part1.title: Core Theory - Sparse Matrix Concepts
topics: Why sparse matrices for CFD: structured vs unstructured meshes
Dense vs sparse storage: memory complexity analysis
Memory efficiency calculations for large CFD problems

part2.title: Physical Challenge - Memory vs Speed Tradeoffs
topics: Memory requirements for R410A evaporator simulation

part3.title: Architecture & Implementation - OpenFOAM lduMatrix Framework
topics: lduMatrix class hierarchy and nested classes
lduAddressing: owner, neighbour, and losort addressing

critical_notes: lduMatrix stores three coefficient arrays: lowerPtr_, diagPtr_, upperPtr_
Matrix-vector product Amul computes result[i] = diag[i]*psi[i] + sum of face contributions
LDU format achieves ~3N storage vs N^2 for dense (where N=cells, ~7N faces)
diagonalPreconditioner stores reciprocal diagonal (rD) for faster multiplication
lduAddressing uses demand-driven (lazy) evaluation for losort and ownerStart arrays

key_formulas.name: Sparse Storage Memory Ratio
key_formulas.description: For unstructured meshes with ~7N faces, LDU stores ~3N coefficients

lduMatrix.name: lduMatrix
lduMatrix.description: General matrix class with coefficients stored as three arrays (lower, diagonal, upper)

data_members.lowerPtr_: scalarField pointer to lower triangle coefficients
data_members.upperPtr_: scalarField pointer to upper triangle coefficients

solver.description: Base class for lduMatrix solvers (PCG, PBiCGStab, GAMG)

smoother.description: Base class for smoothers (used in multigrid)

lduAddressing.name: lduAddressing
lduAddressing.description: Addressing required by lduMatrix: upper, lower, and losort

data_members.rD: scalarField - reciprocal diagonal (stored because '*' is faster than '/')

methods.precondition: Apply diagonal preconditioning: wA = rD * rA

learning_objectives: Understand why sparse matrices are essential for large-scale CFD simulations
Explain the LDU (Lower-Diagonal-Upper) storage format and its memory efficiency
Derive the matrix-vector multiplication algorithm for sparse LDU format
Navigate OpenFOAM's lduMatrix class hierarchy and understand its architecture
Explain the role of preconditioning in iterative solver convergence
Apply linear algebra concepts to R410A evaporator simulation requirements

connections_to_other_days.from_day_02: Builds on Gauss divergence theorem and discrete operators
connections_to_other_days.from_day_05: Requires understanding of mesh topology (owner-neighbour connectivity)
connections_to_other_days.from_day_06: Matrix assembly uses boundary condition coefficients
connections_to_other_days.to_day_08: LDU matrices are solved by iterative solvers covered next
connections_to_other_days.to_day_21: Pressure-velocity coupling requires efficient linear solvers
connections_to_other_days.to_day_45: Two-phase flow equations create large sparse systems

solver_selection.energy_equation: PCG with GAMG for large temperature fields

concept_check: Explain why CFD matrices are sparse
Compare dense vs sparse memory requirements for N=10^6 cells
Derive the LDU matrix-vector product formula
Explain the trade-offs between different preconditioners
code_verification: Locate lduMatrix class definition in source code
Verify Amul implementation matches derived formula
Trace how owner/neighbour addressing is used
practical_application: Calculate memory requirements for R410A evaporator case
Analyze impact of mesh quality on matrix properties
